intro: Humata cicutarioides, also known as Ceylonese paku or Ceylonese fern, is a species of plant in the family Lomariopsidaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in Southeast Asian medicine for its various health benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The bioactive constituents of Humata cicutarioides include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Humata cicutarioides is often made into herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tonics, which are used to promote health and well-being.
